data.body: E2 round-2 caught a REAL isolation bug (G6 impact 1.0): plan's 'cp .tickets-tracker -> TMP_MUTANT_STORE' is NOT isolated because .tickets-tracker is a git LINKED WORKTREE (its .git is a file-pointer to the real object store; init.py:218,347-367) -> a cp'd clone's commits resolve to and MUTATE the real tickets branch (REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off only blocks push, not local commit). Fix: use 'git clone --local --branch tickets REAL_REPO TMP' (own .git DIR) + assert isdir(TMP/.git) BEFORE injection (structural prevention, not post-hoc detection). Applied to E2 plan (3 edits) -> re-review round 3 running. PROPAGATED the same fix to the E6 + E3 build subagents (E3 especially, since it deletes sidecars from its clone). The plan-review gate earning its keep — caught a bug that would've corrupted the live store. Also flagged to E3 subagent: verify sidecar-deletion actually neutralizes Pass-2 recall (gate may read sidecars from the branch, not just working tree).
